question: Do repeat customers spend more per order than first-time buyers?
sql: WITH ranked AS (SELECT user_id, order_id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY MIN(created_at)) AS order_rank, SUM(sale_price) AS order_value FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.order_items` WHERE status IN ('Complete', 'Shipped') GROUP BY user_id, order_id) SELECT IF(order_rank = 1, 'first order', 'repeat order') AS order_type, ROUND(AVG(order_value), 2) AS avg_order_value, COUNT(*) AS orders FROM ranked GROUP BY order_type
analyst_notes: Rank each user's orders by time (ROW_NUMBER over MIN(created_at) per order), then compare first vs subsequent order values. Aggregate to order level BEFORE averaging — item-level averages weight big orders wrong.
author: claude-draft
